rulings.Case Title: HOWARD I. MAIBACH VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Application For Leave To File Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 8, RESPONDENT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Notice Of Application For Leave To File Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16 Judge Harold Kahn, who is assigned to hear all motions in Department 301 on January 23, 2025, recuses himself from hearing this case. The hearing on this motion is continued to January 30, 2025 to be heard by a judge other than Judge Kahn. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EASTON RUHNKE ET AL VS. HUBBELL POWER SYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 9, DEFENDANT PRO ENERGY SERVICES GROUP, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Defendant Pro Energy Services Group, LLC's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. Even assuming that Pro Energy satisfied its initial summary motion burden on either or both issues, which is doubtful, plaintiffs Easton and Kayla Ruhnke presented admissible evidence showing that there are triable disputes whether: 1) Pro Energy Group is an "affiliated company" of Quanta Energy Services within the meaning of the quoted phrase in the Quanta release and 2) Randy Goforth was a special employee of PAR and Pro Energy did not have the ability and did not exercise any control over his work. (Plaintiffs' Responses to Defendant's Asserted Material Facts and Plaintiffs' Additional Material Facts). Notwithstanding Pro Energy's failure to obtain leave to have this motion heard 30 days before trial, the court chose to adjudicate the merits of the motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: NORMA CRISTINA VERDUZCO VS. ERIC CRESPO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Against Plaintiff Norma Cristina Verduzco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 1, DEFENDANT WISE CHOICE TRANS CORP., INC'S. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Against Plaintiff Norma Cristina Verduzco Defendant Wise Choice Trans Corp., Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication in its favor of plaintiff Norma Verduzco's eleventh through fourteenth causes of action is granted and its motion for summary adjudication as to Ms. Verduzco's request for punitive damages as to those claims is off calendar as moot. Wise Choice satisfied its initial summary motion burden as to the eleventh through fourteenth causes of action by presenting admissible evidence that it terminated Ms. Verduzco's employment based on its honest belief that Ms. Verduzco was working for a competitive company while she was on disability leave. Ms. Verduzco failed to present any evidence that the reason given for the termination of her employment was pretextual or that she was terminated in whole or in part due to her claimed disability, to retaliate against her for taking a medical leave or for any other impermissible discriminatory reason or any reason that violates California public policy. The adjudication of the eleventh through fourteenth causes against Ms. Verduzco moots the portion of this motion seeking summary adjudication of Ms. Verduzco's request for punitive damages on those claims. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ADAM O'CONNOR VS. SCOTT NEWLIN JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Undertaking [Code Civ. Proc. 1030]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 6, DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE AMBULANCE SERVICE, INC.") Motion For Undertaking [Code Civ. Proc. 1030] Defendant's motion for an undertaking per CCP 1030 is denied. To prevail on this motion, defendant must establish that plaintiff resides out of state and "there is a reasonable possibility that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in the action." (CCP 1030(b).) Defendant fails to show a reasonable possibility of obtaining judgment. The evidence discloses that after plaintiff made his lane change, defendant's driver accelerated. (Buckley Decl., Ex. C [Yamaguchi Depo., 50:17-51:15].) There is therefore evidence of comparative fault and it is unlikely that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in its favor. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JERMILA MCCOY VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With The Courts November 1, 2024 Discovery Order, And Request For Monetary And Issue Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 1, PLAINTIFF JERMILA MCCOY'S Motion To Compel Compliance With The Courts November 1, 2024 Discovery Order, And Request For Monetary And Issue Sanctions Plaintiff Jermila McCoy's motion to compel compliance with the November 1 discovery order and for monetary and issue sanctions is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff acknowledges in her reply brief that Defendant has now fully complied with the November 1 order, but still seeks monetary and issue sanctions. Both monetary and issue sanctions are denied because defendant American Honda's conduct did not lack substantial justification. American Honda's delayed compliance is understandable given the large quantity of documents it produced. Because trial is not for almost eight months, the court discerns no prejudice to the plaintiff due to the delayed compliance. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. HIGHFIELD HOSPITALITY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Facts Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 3, PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S Motion To Deem Facts Admitted Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to deem facts admitted is denied for two separate and independent reasons.. First, plaintiff prematurely served the requests for admissions. Plaintiff served the discovery on September 17, 2024. (Ray Decl., par. 5.) Service of the discovery was premature since plaintiff had yet to effectuate service of the summons and complaint, which occurred on September 22, 2024. (Ray Decl., par. 6, Ex. B.) "A plaintiff may make requests for admission by a party without leave of court at any time that is 10 days after the service of the summons on, or appearance by, that party, whichever occurs first." (CCP 2033.020(b).) The claimed service of the original complaint on July 11, 2024,did not comply with CCP 415.20(a). That provision allows a plaintiff to serve a corporation by leaving a copy of the summons and complaint at the office of the agent for service of process, president, etc. or that individual's usual mailing address. (See CCP 416.10.) Here, plaintiff served a bartender at defendant's cocktail bar. (Ray Decl., par. 4.) There is no showing that the cocktail bar is the office or usual mailing address of one of the individuals identified in CCP 416.10. Second, the motion is moot because defendant Highfield Hospitality provided responses on December 13, 2024. (Ray Decl., par. 12, Ex. C.) Both sides' request for monetary sanctions are denied since neither side's position lacked substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JIAMEI ZHOU ET AL VS. LAWRENCE YANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Jiamei Zhou, Chuxin Zhou, Xiaoting Liu, And Zishao Zhous Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories Numbers 2, 5, 6, 7, 27, 31, 35, 39, 43, 47, 51, 55, 58, 62, 67, 78
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, January 23, 2025, line 4 DEFENDANTS LAWRENCE YANG, ANNIE YANG'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Jiamei Zhou, Chuxin Zhou, Xiaoting Liu, And Zishao Zhous Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories Numbers 2, 5, 6, 7, 27, 31, 35, 39, 43, 47, 51, 55, 58, 62, 67, 78 Defendants Lawrence and Annie Yang's motion to compel plaintiffs to provide further responses to certain special interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety. The sole issue raised in the defendants' initial memorandum is the plaintiffs' use of the very common phrase "including, but not limited to." Defendants provide no authority or explanation why the use of that phrase is problematic in the context of plaintiffs' discovery responses or that they made anything me than a perfunctory effort to address their concerns with the use of that phrase. This is particularly troubling in light of the large amount of monetary sanctions sought by defendants. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

Both parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than January 31, 2025. = (302/HEK)
